33.text: This Law may be cited as the “Limited Liability Partnership Law 2004".

35.text: This Law is made by the Ruler of Dubai.

37.text: This Law applies in the jurisdiction of the Dubai International Financial Centre.

39.text: This Law is enacted on the date specified in the Enactment Notice in respect of this Law.

41.text: This Law comes into force on the date specified in the Enactment Notice in respect of this Law.

44.text: (a) interpretative provisions which apply to this Law; and (b) a list of defined terms used in this Law.

47.text: This Law and any legislation made for the purpose of this Law is administered by the Registrar.

55.text: (1) The Limited Liability Partnership Agreement shall be in the English language and shall be Printed and be divided into paragraphs numbered consecutively.

56.text: (2) The mutual rights and duties of the Limited Liability Partnership and the Members shall be determined, subject to the terms of the Limited Liability Partnership Agreement, by the following rules:

57.text:
(a) all Members of a Limited Liability Partnership are entitled to share equally in the capital and profits of the Limited Liability Partnership.

58.text: (c) every Member may take part in the management of the Limited Liability Partnership.

59.text: no Member shall be entitled to remuneration for acting in the business or management of the Limited Liability Partnership.

60.text: (e) no person may be introduced as a Member or voluntarily assign an interest in a Limited Liability Partnership without the consent of all existing Members.

61.text: (f) any difference arising as to ordinary matters connected with the business of the Limited Liability Partnership shall be decided by a majority of the Members, but no change may be made in the natur...

63.text: (h) each Member shall render true accounts and full information of all things affecting the Limited Liability Partnership to any Member or his legal representatives.

64.text: (i) if a Member, without the consent of the Limited Liability Partnership, carries on any business of the same nature as and competing with the Limited Liability Partnership, he shall account for and ...

69.text: (1) On the registration of a Limited Liability Partnership and its Limited Liability Partnership Agreement the Registrar shall:

70.text: (a) give a certificate that the Limited Liability Partnership is incorporated; and (b) allocate to the Limited Liability Partnership a number, which shall be the Limited Liability Partnership’s regist...

71.text: (2) From the date of incorporation mentioned in the certificate of incorporation, the Members shall be a body corporate having the name contained in the certificate of incorporation capable forthwith ...

72.text: (3) A certificate of incorporation is conclusive evidence:

73.text: (a) of the incorporation of the Limited Liability Partnership; and (b) that the requirements of this Law have been complied with in respect of the registration of the Limited Liability Partnership.

74.text: PART 3: ADMINISTRATION OF AFFAIRS OF A LIMITED LIABILITY PARTNERSHIP

76.text: A Limited Liability Partnership shall hold a Licence pursuant to Article 9 of the Operating Law.

77.text: 13. Conduct of Business in the DIFC

78.text: A Limited Liability Partnership shall, comply with the requirements of this Law and Part 3 of the Operating Law in respect to the conduct of its business in the DIFC.

92.text: (4) A Member of a Limited Liability Partnership shall not be regarded for any purpose as employed by the Limited Liability Partnership.

93.text: (5) If a Limited Liability Partnership carries on business without having at least two (2) Members and does so for more than 14 days then:

123.text: (1) If the Limited Liability Partnership Agreement specifies who are to be Designated Members:

124.text:
(a) they are Designated Members on incorporation; and   
(b) any Member may become a Designated Member by and in accordance with an agreement with the other Members,

125.text: and a Member may cease to be a designated Member in accordance with an agreement with the other Members.

126.text: (2) There must be at least one Designated Member, and where no Member is appointed, then every Member is a Designated Member.

129.text: (a) notice that specified Members are to be Designated Members; or (b) notice that every person who from time to time is a Member of the Limited Liability Partnership is a Designated Member;

130.text: and once it is delivered, Article 23(1) and Article 23(2) or Article 23(3), shall have effect as if that were stated in the Limited Liability Partnership Agreement.

182.text: (1) A Member of a Limited Liability Partnership shall not knowingly or recklessly make to the Limited Liability Partnership's auditor, either orally or in writing, a statement which:

183.text: (a) conveys or purports to convey any information or explanation which the auditor requires, or is entitled to require, as auditor of the Limited Liability Partnership; and

185.text: (i) false, misleading or deceptive in a material particular; or (ii) is such that it omits information where the omission of such information is likely to mislead or deceive the auditor.

191.text: (1) An auditor shall disclose to the Registrar any material matters concerning the accounts of a Limited Liability Partnership which reasonably tend to show that there has been a failure or a likely f...

192.text: (2) Without limiting the application of Article 34(1) or any other provision of this Law, an auditor does not contravene any duty to which the auditor is subject merely because the auditor gives to th...

193.text: (a) a notification as required under Article 34(1); or (b) any other information or opinion;

194.text: if the auditor is acting in good faith and reasonably believes that the notification, information or opinion is relevant to any functions of the Registrar.

196.text: 35. Foreign Limited Liability Partnerships (1) A Foreign Limited Liability Partnership shall not carry on business in the DIFC unless:

197.text:
(a) it is registered as a Recognised Limited Liability Partnership under this Part; or   
(b) it has submitted all necessary Documents and information to be so registered in accordance with the requir...

198.text: (2) The Board of Directors of the DIFCA may make Regulations prescribing what comprises ‘carrying on business’ for the purposes of this Part.

199.text: (3) A Foreign Limited Liability Partnership may apply to the Registrar for registration as a Recognised Limited Liability Partnership in such manner as shall be Prescribed in the Regulations.

200.text: 36. Requirements of a Recognised Limited Liability Partnership

201.text: (1) A Recognised Limited Liability Partnership shall:

202.text: (a) appoint and retain at all times at least one person who is authorised to accept service of any Document or notice on behalf of the Recognised Limited Liability Partnership and to undertake any oth...

203.text: (b) have a principal place of business in the DIFC to which all communications and notices may be addressed;

204.text: (c) hold a Licence pursuant to Article 9 of the Operating Law, unless exempted by the Registrar; and

205.text: (d) file with the Registrar notice of:

207.text: in the form and manner required in the Regulations.

208.text: (2) The Board of Directors of the DIFCA may make Regulations:

209.text: (a) prescribing procedures in relation to requirements under this Part; and (b) waiving or modifying any requirements under this Part in relation to different cases or classes of cases.

210.text: 37. Conduct of business in the DIFC

211.text: A Recognised Limited Liability Partnership shall comply with the requirements of this Law and where applicable, Part 3 of the Operating Law, in respect to the conduct of its business in the DIFC.

213.text: A Recognised Limited Liability Partnership shall keep Accounting Records including underlying Documents which are sufficient to show and explain its transactions so as to:

214.text:
(a) disclose with reasonable accuracy the financial position of the Recognised Limited Liability Partnership at any time; and   
(b) enable the partners to ensure that any accounts prepared by the Rec...

215.text: (2) A Recognised Limited Liability Partnership’s Accounting Records shall be:

216.text:
(a) kept at such place as the partners think fit unless specifically Prescribed in the Regulations;   
(b) preserved by the Recognised Limited Liability Partnership for at least six (6) years from the...

219.text: Part 5 of the Operating Law applies to the Limited Liability Partnership or Recognised Limited Liability Partnership in respect of the powers and remedies of the Registrar.

221.text: The provision of Articles 32 and 33 of the Operating Law apply to a Limited Liability Partnership and so far as the Law allows, a Recognised Limited Liability Partnership.

235.text: 43. Waivers and Modification of Law or Regulations

236.text: The powers to waive and modify the Operating Law and Regulations contained in Article 60 of the Operating Law, shall apply to this Law and Regulations made thereunder.

238.text: (1) The Board of Directors of the DIFCA may make Regulations requiring the payment to the Registrar of such fees as may be Prescribed in respect of:

239.text: (a) the performance by the Registrar of such functions under this Law as may be specified in the Regulations, including the receipt by him of any Document under this Law which is required to be delive...

240.text: (2) The Registrar may charge a fee for any services provided by him otherwise than in pursuance of an obligation imposed on him by this Law.

241.text: (3) Where a fee is provided for or charged under this Article for the performance of an act or duty by the Registrar, no action need be taken by him until the fee is paid, and where the fee is payable...

245.text:

246.text: (2) The headings in the Law shall not affect its interpretation.

247.text: (3) References in this Law to a body corporate include a body corporate incorporated outside DIFC.

248.text: (4) A reference in this Law to a Part, Article or Schedule by number only, and without further identification, is a reference to the Part, Article or Schedule of that number in this Law.
